rounds_summary.attempted_route: pandas boolean masking on Quality/Classification/type_Ki/type_KD/Organism; dropped Classification and Organism columns before saving
rounds_summary.observation: Script exited 0, produced 336 rows, scored 0/336; evaluator expected Classification and Organism columns in output that the script had dropped

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Same pandas masking logic but merged Classification/Organism into df and retained them; no row explosion; multi-assay rows kept intact with semicolon-delimited fields
rounds_summary.observation: Produced 336 rows, scored 0/292; evaluator reference uses per-assay exploded rows, so semicolon-packed rows never matched any reference row

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Custom explode_multiassay_row() splits semicolon-delimited rows before filtering; pd.to_numeric() cast type_* columns; dropped Classification/Organism if absent from unfiltered
rounds_summary.observation: Produced 345 rows, scored 0/336; diff tuples show float vs int mismatch (1.0 vs 1) in type_* cols; Classification/Organism again dropped from output

ruled_out_routes: pandas masking without row explosion: semicolon-packed multi-assay rows never match per-assay reference rows regardless of column set
pandas merge + retain Classification/Organism without explosion: correct columns but wrong row granularity, score 0/292
custom explode_multiassay_row() with pd.to_numeric() cast: produces float dtype (1.0) for type_* columns, failing exact-match comparison against integer reference values (1)
conditionally dropping Classification/Organism based on presence in unfiltered: reference expects these columns present; any conditional drop logic reintroduces the round-1 column-missing failure
synthesised_diagnosis: All three rounds fail because the evaluator applies exact row-by-row equality across columns, dtype, and granularity simultaneously. Round 1 failed on missing columns; round 2 fixed columns but failed on row granularity (multi-assay vs single-assay); round 3 fixed granularity but failed on dtype (float 1.0 from pd.to_numeric vs int 1 in reference) and reintroduced the missing-column bug. Each fix corrected one axis while breaking or leaving another, revealing that the evaluation function is a strict schema fingerprint check requiring correct columns, per-assay row granularity, AND original integer dtypes simultaneously.
rationale: The evaluator performs exact-match comparison on every field; the three axes — column set, row granularity, and dtype — are independent requirements that must ALL be satisfied together. Each round fixed one axis while leaving or reintroducing failures on the others, producing a 0 score despite progressively closer outputs.
recommended_alternative: Explode semicolon-delimited rows using pandas str.split + DataFrame.explode on the AID column (not a custom loop), then for type_* columns use pd.to_numeric(..., errors='coerce').astype('Int64') to produce nullable integer dtype matching the reference. Merge Classification and Organism from protein_set and unconditionally retain them in the final output (do not conditionally drop). Use df.reset_index(drop=True) before saving.
